content:
The triage/agent orchestration model is based on behaviour trees (from game AI). Key concepts from the user's blog post:
- Agent runs on a loop; first prompt is to run setup, read inbox and next action (deterministic instructions at every pass)
- Two zones: urgent/reactive (failing tests, critiques, inbox messages) at the top, proactive (explore, prioritise, execute) at the bottom — Maslow's hierarchy analogy
- Separation of stages prevents agents skipping steps in the name of efficiency
- Agents are envisioned as pure functions: take instructions, make modifications, no side effects
- Explore phase uses deterministic inputs: code health checks (Octoclean), invariant checking (spec vs implementation vs tests), inspiration
- Tree structure: Selector with serial evaluation — tests failing → fix, unresolved critiques → fix, unreviewed commits → review, inbox → handle, work-item exists → execute, candidates exist → prioritise, etc.
- Runs in a daily branch (designed for overnight autonomous work), can be deleted if unwanted.

provenance.raw_agent: I can't browse URLs directly — I don't have internet access. Could you paste the content of the post?
